overview: The witest28-6 system consists of exactly one component — hello-world-server — with no external dependencies on other services. Because there are no inter-component relationships to sequence, the entire system is implemented in a single phase. This ordering is trivially optimal and allows the full application to be built, tested, and deployed in one continuous effort.

phases.name: Foundation & Delivery
phases.description: The sole component of the system — a self-contained Go HTTP server — is implemented in full. It has no upstream dependencies, so it can be built and deployed immediately without waiting on any other service.

components.name: hello-world-server
responsibilities: Serve a well-formed, accessible HTML 'Hello, World!' page at the root path (/) with HTTP 200.
Return a user-friendly HTML 404 response for all undefined routes via the ServeMux catch-all.
Expose a /health endpoint returning HTTP 200 with JSON body {"status": "ok"} for liveness probes.
Apply security response headers (Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy) and suppress server version disclosure on every response.
Emit structured access logs (ISO 8601 timestamp, HTTP method, path, status code) to stdout for every request.
components.architectureContext: This is the only component in the system. It is called directly by end-users (browsers) and by Kubernetes liveness/readiness probes via /health. It makes no outbound calls to any other service, database, or external resource. All routing, security hardening, and observability are handled entirely within this single binary.
keyConsiderations: Use a responseWriterWrapper struct to capture the downstream status code before logging — Go's http.ResponseWriter does not expose the written status code natively.

Register /health on the mux before the root catch-all ('/') to ensure it takes priority; Go's ServeMux routes the most specific pattern first, but explicit registration order matters for clarity and correctness.
The multi-stage Dockerfile (golang:1.22-alpine → scratch/distroless) must produce a statically linked binary (CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux) to avoid glibc issues in the minimal runtime image.

Suppress the Server header by setting it to an empty string in the security middleware after the downstream handler writes; alternatively wrap ResponseWriter to intercept and strip it before flushing.

PORT env var validation is intentionally minimal — a non-numeric value will cause ListenAndServe to fail fast with a logged error, which is acceptable; do not add silent fallback logic that could mask misconfiguration.
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: witest28-6 Hello World API
  description: Minimal Hello World web application serving an HTML page and health endpoint.
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Hello World page
      description: Returns the Hello World HTML page with HTTP 200.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML page with Hello World greeting.
          headers:
            Content-Security-Policy:
              schema:
                type: string
            X-Frame-Options:
              schema:
                type: string
            X-Content-Type-Options:
              schema:
                type: string
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      description: Returns service health status.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /{path}:
    get:
      summary: Catch-all 404
      description: Returns a user-friendly 404 page for any undefined route.
      parameters:
        - name: path
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '404':
          description: Page not found.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
